title: Efficient Transformer Architectures for Long-Context Reasoning
abstract: We propose a novel transformer architecture that achieves linear complexity for long-context reasoning tasks while maintaining competitive performance with quadratic-complexity baselines. Our approach leverages a combination of local and global attention mechanisms with a learned routing strategy.

title: Self-Supervised Representation Learning with Adaptive Masking
abstract: We introduce an adaptive masking strategy for self-supervised representation learning that dynamically adjusts the masking ratio based on input complexity. Our method outperforms fixed-ratio masking on multiple downstream tasks.

title: A Survey of Neural Architecture Search: Methods, Benchmarks, and Applications
abstract: This comprehensive survey covers the latest advances in neural architecture search, including one-shot methods, differentiable approaches, and hardware-aware NAS. We benchmark popular methods on standardized tasks and discuss open challenges.

title: Bridging Vision and Language: A Unified Framework for Multimodal Understanding
venue: Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
abstract: We present a unified framework for multimodal understanding that jointly processes visual and textual inputs through a shared representation space. Our model achieves state-of-the-art results on VQA, image captioning, and cross-modal retrieval benchmarks.

title: Structured Pruning for Efficient Deep Learning Inference
abstract: We propose a structured pruning method that identifies and removes redundant network components while maintaining accuracy. Our approach reduces inference latency by 3x on standard hardware with less than 1% accuracy degradation.

title: Communication-Efficient Federated Learning with Adaptive Compression
abstract: We present an adaptive compression scheme for federated learning that dynamically adjusts compression rates based on gradient importance, significantly reducing communication costs.

title: Understanding Scaling Laws in Deep Learning: A Theoretical Perspective
venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
abstract: We provide theoretical foundations for understanding scaling laws in deep learning, deriving closed-form expressions for the relationship between model size, dataset size, and performance.
